def: s.m. = kāje, and kāraṇ, qq.v.
s.m. pl. (Braj pl. of kāj), Works; occupations; affairs.
s.m. = kājal, q.v.
s.m. (rustic) = kajjal or kājal.
s.m. A bad reputation, bad name, disrepute ( = ku-yaś).
s.m. A bad reputation, bad name, disrepute ( = ku-yaś).
s.m. A camel's saddle; a pair of dorsers or panniers (for a camel).
postpn. See s.v. kāj.
adj. & s.m. Active, assiduous, industrious, laborious, busy, employed, occupied; — a worker, an industrious person; an artisan.

ety: kaj, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Crookedness, curvature, bend; crossness; perverseness.
s.m. Lamp-black, &c. ( = kājal, q.v.).
s.m. Lamp-black, &c. ( = kājal, q.v.).
v.n. To smoulder; to grow dim; — to moulder; to become mouldy or mildewed.
s.f. A vessel or pot for making and keeping lamp-black (kājal); an iron instrument like snuffers, used to receive smoke, and to prepare and keep lamp-black.
s.f. = kajjal, or kājal, q.v.
s.f. = kajrī, q.v.
see s.v. kaj.
s.m. = kajjal or kājal, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀮 (kajjala) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit कज्जल (kajjala).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Black (eyes) without painting.
s.f. A class of songs sung, and a kind of game played, by women in the evenings of the rainy season.

ety: prob. fr. the Port.; cf. Fr. acajou
def: s.m. The cashew nut, Anacardium occidentale.
wety: Borrowed from Portuguese caju, borrowed from Old Tupi akaîu, from Proto-Tupi-Guarani *akaju. First attested in c. 1708.

def: adj. & s.m. Showy but frail, pretty but flimsy (work), gimcrack; flimsy, frail, unsubstantial; — ginger-bread work.
adj. Dried up, dry; gaunt, lank, lean, thin; feeble; — disproportionately long or tall.
s.m. Anything dried (as meat, &c.); dry, hard bread; biscuit; a sweetened cake of bread (offered at a shrine); — bran; — the pupil of the eye; — adj. Small, minute.
s.m. Cork; a cork.
s.f. Cawing (of a crow, &c.)

s.m. (in Pers.), An old slave (belonging to one's father); an elder brother; — (in Hind.) a father's younger brother; a paternal uncle; a respectful compellation for an elder brother, or cousin, &c., and for the younger sons and grandsons of a Sikh Rājā or Sardār.
s.m. (f. -ī), A crow
subs: s.f. A woman that bears only one child
s.m. A kind of mountain crow, a large crow; a raven; a r̤ishi or god of crows who (in the form of a crow) ate the leavings of Rām
s.m. 'Crow's feather'; the side-locks which are left when the head (of a Hindū) is first shaved; side-locks; ringlets, tresses

adj. & s.m. Unexpected, accidental, casual, fortuitous (as in the fable of the fruit of the plam falling unexpectedly at the moment of the alighting of a crow, and killing it); — coincidence, accident; chance, casualty
s.f. The plant Menispermum cocculus (the seed of which is the Cocculus indicus)
s.m. The colocynth or bitter apple, Cucumis colocynthis.

def: s.m. A paternal uncle.

ety: prob. corr. fr. kākaḍ-singī = kākṛā-singī, q.v.
def: s.f. (dialec.), Ink-nut flowers.
s.m. The (Hindī) alphabet; an alphabet.
s.m. A kind of leprosy with black and red spots (considered incurable).
s.f. A species of small grain, Panicum italicum; millet ( = kaṅgnī).
A ring worn on the wrist, a bracelet.
s.f. Name of a country; — Emblic myrobalan.
A ring worn on the wrist, a bracelet.
s.m. The letter क k
s.m. A cockatoo, a species of large parrot (found in the Malayan peninsula, the eastern isles, &c.).
s.m. = کاکاتوا kākā-tūʼā.
s.f. corr. of kāk-bandhyā, q.v.s.v. kāka or kāk.
s.m. An upper story; a gallery; a balcony; a palace; a villa; a tower or turret; battlements.
s.f. The armpit
subs: s.f. The space from the shoulder to the armpit
s.f. A painful suppurating tumour in the armpit.

ety: S. कक्ष+पोटिका or स्फोटिका; — or kākh, q.v.+Prk. अउल्लिआ = S. क+उल+कः
def: s.f. A boil, or painful suppurating tumour, in the armpit.

ety: S. कक्ष+पोटिका or स्फोटिका; — or kākh, q.v.+Prk. अउल्लिआ = S. क+उल+कः
def: s.f. A boil, or painful suppurating tumour, in the armpit.
s.f. A bundle of sheaves of corn (as much as can be carried under the arm) given to each reaper and village servant.

ety: S. कक्ष + dim. aff. rī = Prk. डिआ = S. र+इका
def: s.f. The armpit; — soreness of the armpit.

ety: S. कक्ष+पोटिका or स्फोटिका; — or kākh, q.v.+Prk. अउल्लिआ = S. क+उल+कः
def: s.f. A boil, or painful suppurating tumour, in the armpit.
s.f. The wife of a paternal uncle, an aunt.
s.f. A crow.
s.f. A particular small coin; a small sum of money equal to twenty cowries; — the cowrie-shell (Cyprœa moneta) used as a coin; — a cubit.
s.m. The letter क k
s.m. Cut tobacco; — a kind of ḥuqqa with a long tube
subs: s.m. A great smoker of the kakkaṛ
s.m. A house where people smoke
s.m. A pull at the ḥuqqa called kakkaṛ
s.m. One who supplies travellers, &c. with a ḥuqqa for smoking.

def: s.f. = kāklūt, q.v.
s.f. (dialec.) Soft and tender melting; attachment, passion; desire, longing; avidity, greediness, stinginess, niggardliness.
adj. & s.m. Attached; impassioned; desirous; covetous, avaricious; — a tender lover, an impassioned or eager lover; — a miser.
s.f. The plant Anamirta coculus; also Coculus indicus.
s.m. A fabulous bird, the phœnix.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ققنس (qaqnus).

def: s.m. A fabulous bird, the phœnix.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قُقنُوس, from Arabic قُقْنُس (quqnus).

def: s.m. A raven; — a poisonous substance of a black colour (possibly the berry of the Cocculus indicus); — name of one of the divisions of the infernal regions.
s.f. Name of a root or vegetable substance used in medicine (described as sweet and cooling, allaying fever, removing phlegm, &c. It is said to be brought from Nepaul or Morung).
s.m. A kind of vegetable, Momordica muricata; also M. mixta.
v.t. To scratch, scrape; to scoop, hollow, excavate.
s.m. Stiff or firm leather; a strap of leather; thong (of a whip); — a large or stiff wick.
s.f. (dialec.) = kakhaurī or kakhaulī, qq.v.
s.f. (dialec.) = kakhaurī or kakhaulī, qq.v.

s.f. The tree Pistacia (or Rhus) integerrima (the fruit of which is compared to the claw or feelers of a crab); one of the gall-like excrescences formed on the leaves and petioles of the same plant, and used in native medicine.

s.f. The tree Pistacia (or Rhus) integerrima (the fruit of which is compared to the claw or feelers of a crab); one of the gall-like excrescences formed on the leaves and petioles of the same plant, and used in native medicine.
s.m. corr. of kākrez, q.v.
s.m. Dark purple colour.
adj. Dark purple; purple-coloured.
s.f. 1˚ = kakhrī, q.v.; — 2˚ = kakṛī, q.v.
s.f. A kind of cucumber, Cucumis utilissimus.
s.m. Armpit, side, flank (see kāṅkh).
s.f. A waistband, girdle; the end of the lower garment, which, after the cloth is carried round the body, is brought up behind and tucked into the waistband.
s.m. A paternal uncle ( = kākā, q.v.); — sarcasm.
s.f. Space, region, quarter (as east, west, &c.).
s.m. Summit (of a mountain); hump (of a bull, &c.); an ensign or symbol of royalty (as the white parasol, &c.).
s.f. A curl, lock, ringlet; a tuft of hair left on the top of the head
s.m. Large cardamoms.
s.m. A fine kind of ermine.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قاقم (qāqum).

def: s.f. Ranunculus.
s.m. A saying, a word; loquaciousness; — boasting, egotism
subs: v.n. To talk, prate; to be very loquacious; — (with loc.) to rebuke, reproach
or qīl-o-qāl, s.m. Confabulation; logomachy; altercation, wrangling, squabbling.

def: s.m. Bad or inauspicious time; dearth, famine; calamity; — adj. Bad, inauspicious; unseasonable
subs: Famine to happen; the coming on of famine
or kāl-kā mārā, adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Famine-stricken; — a victim of famine; a starveling

def: adv. (Braj, & rustic) To-morrow, &c. ( = kal, q.v.).
s.m. & adv. The morrow, to-morrow; yesterday ("in the Dakkhanī dialect kal is used only in the sense of 'yesterday'; 'to-morrow' is expressed by ṣabāṅ or ṣubāṅ," corr. fr. A. ṣabāḥ, or ṣubḥ)
subs: adj. (f. -kī), Of or relating to yesterday
A man of yesterday, novus homo, an upstart, a parvenu
A matter of yesterday, a recent occurrence
or kal-rāt-ko, Last night; to-morrow night
adv. & conj. In future; perchance; lest.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.f. A small part (of anything), part, side (e.g. dekhiye ūṅṭ kis kal baiṭhtā hai); — a machine, or a part of one; an instrument; a lock; a trap; a trigger, hammer (of a gun)
subs: adj. & s.m. Formed or wrought by machinery; having a milled rim (a coin); having a trigger (a gun); — coin with a milled edge, a rupee, &c.; a trigger-gun, an English gun (opp. to toṛe-dār, 'a matchlock')
adj. (f. -kī), Made by machinery; worked by machinery; mechanical
An automaton, a puppet; (met.) a very weak person
A workshop in which machinery is employed
A mechanical horse; a well-trained and obedient horse
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.f. Ease, tranquillity, peace, quiet, relief, rest, repose
subs: or kal paṛnā (-ko), or kal pānā, To obtain ease or relief; to be at ease
or kal-se be-kal honā, To be ill at ease, to be disturbed or disquieted, to lose all rest or peace
s.f. Uneasiness, perturbation, disquietude, distraction, trouble.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.f. = kalī or kali, q.v.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.m. = کلي kali, q.v.: — kal-jug = kali-yug.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: adj. & s.m. Scald-headed; bald; — a man who has wounds and scars on his head and has lost his hair; a bald-head.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Black, dark; — great, fearful, terrible; — a name of Krishṇ; the black and most venomous variety of the cobra, Coluber naga; — a sepoy
subs: or kāle-bāl, s.m. lit. 'Black hair or hairs,' the pubes; the groin; — kālā-bāl apnā jānnā or samajhnā (-ko), To hold (one) in no esteem, or in great contempt
s.m. A black snake; — adj. Black as a snake, very black, jet black
s.m. lit. 'Black water'; beyond the sea; transportation (across the sea)
s.m. A black mountain or hill; (met.) an elephant; — the head
s.m. A great thief; — an unknown person; a dark horse
s.m. Name of a purgative seed, Ipomæa cœrulea; indigo seed; woad, Convolvulus nil
s.m. The purple thorn-apple, Datura fastuosa purpurascens
s.m. A black demon; a great devil; — a very black man
or kālā jīrā, s.m. Seed of the Nigella indica
v.t. To make black, to blacken; to blot, scribble over, deface by scribbling
adj. (f. -ī), Black as a lamp-black box, coal-black, jet-black
s.m. A black crow, a raven

or muṅh kālā karnā (-kā), To blacken the face (of), to disgrace, bring disgrace (on); to turn out with disgrace, to expel; to break off all connexion (with); — to copulate (with); to violate, to ravish
or kālā non, s.m. A kind of black rock-salt, impregnated with sulphur and bitumen, which leaves a hepatic flavour in the mouth (it is much used medicinally)
s.f. 'A black calamity'; a great evil; a plague; an old hag
adj. Of the colour of a snake; of a black colour
s.f. A rich black soil, vegetable mould
v.n. To look very angry and threatening
s.f. Basil, Ocymum basilicum or sanctum
s.f. A variety of cowhage with a purplish stem, Dolichos (carpopogon) pruriens purpurascens; — kāle-kos, s.m. pl. 'Great distances,' a great distance
s.f. Black soil ( = kālī bhūmi); — black-lead
(-ke), 'To place a black pot on the head' (of); to bring disgrace (upon).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit काल (kāla), from Proto-Dravidian *kār.

def: s.m. Time ( = kāl, q.v.).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit काल (kāla), from Proto-Dravidian *kār.

def: s.f. Name of a daughter of Daksha (the mother of the Kālakeyas, a family of asurs or demons).
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit काल (kāla), from Proto-Dravidian *kār.

def: s.m. = کاله kāla, q.v.
wety: Inherited from Sanskrit काल (kāla), from Proto-Dravidian *kār.

def: s.m. Silk-cloth; cloth; household furniture or utensils, effects, worldly goods, things ( = kālā).

s.f. A sixteenth part (of anything); a small part; part, portion, share; a digit or one-sixteenth of the moon's diameter; a particular division of time (about eight seconds); the sixtieth part of a degree, a minute; interest on capital (considered as a part of it); — any practical art, a mechanical or elegant art; accomplishment, skill, dexterity, ingenuity, art, contrivance, trick, juggling; — (in Hindū poetry) a symbol for the number sixteen
subs: s.f. Tumbling, turning over head and heels, taking a somersault
To tumble, to take a somersault
(S. kalā+jaṅghā), s.f. An acrobatic feat; a trick in wrestling
s.m. 'A treasury or storehouse of digits,' the moon
To disobey, to decline; not to succeed (cf. next)
'A contrivance or device to fail of effect,' not to avail or succeed, not to have an advantage
vulg. kalāṅ-vat, or kalāṅ-vant, s.m. (f. -vatī), lit. 'Possessed of art or skill'; a class of singers or musicians; one of that class, a minstrel; — lit. 'having digits'; the moon.
wety: From Sanskrit कील (kīl).

def: s.m. Time; (in Gram.) tense; season; age; right or proper time, fit season; meal-time; Time personified; destiny, fate; death; time of death; the messenger or angel of death; the regent of the dead, one of the names of Jam or Yama
subs: (-ko), Death to come, the last hour to come (to)
(kāla+an˚), s.m. Intervening time, interval; the lapse of time, the process of time, period; — another time; a more suitable time
To pass time; to spend time, to while away time; to waste time
adj. In the clutches or grasp of death; liable to death; seized by fate; — kāl-bas honā, v.n. To be in the clutches of death or fate, &c.
s.m. 'The dawning of the best season,' the two months following the rainy season; the sultry season; the autumn
vulg. kālagya, s.m. One who knows the fixed times or seasons; an astrologer
vulg. kāl-gyān, s.m. Worldly or secular knowledge, the knowledge of worldly things (as distinguished from things spiritual and eternal)
s.m. The wheel of time; a given revolution of time, a cycle, a period of time, an epoch
s.m. The law, rule, or operation of time; death, dying; — the influence of time; seasonableness

s.f. 'The night of all-destroying time'; a particular night in the life of man, on the seventh day of the seventh month of the seventy-seventh year (after which period a man is exempt from attending to the usual religious ordinances)
s.m. The thread of time or death; name of one of the twenty-one hells
v.n. To go the way of all flesh
s.m. The lapse of time, the process of time
adv. According to the process of time
s.m. Allowing time to pass away, delay, loss of time; spending or passing time; enduring suffering or affliction
v.n. To be subject to time and circumstances; to be in the hands of death ( = kāl-bas honā)
s.f. lit. 'Rope of death'; a snake
s.f. The felly or rim of the wheel of time'; a kind of terrible weapon; — name of an Asur or demon (slain by Krishṇ)
adj. (in Gram.) Denoting time, having reference to time, of time

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Black ( = kālā), of a dark colour, dark; dark-blue; — great, large, excessive, terrible; — a black or dark-blue colour; — the black part of the eye; — the black and most venomous variety of the Cobra, Coluber naga
subs: (˚la+an˚), s.m. A small sbrub used as a purgative
(˚la+ag˚), s.m. A black kind of aloe-wood or Agallochum; — kāl-juʼārī, s.m. A great or terrible gambler
or kāl-sarp, s.m. The black and most poisonous variety of the cobra ( = kāla, or kālā, or kālā sāṅp)
or kāl-ganḍait, s.m. A kind of cobra with white spots
s.m. A kind of black factitious and purgative salt ( = kālā lon, and kālā namak)
s.f. Bengal madder, Rubia munjista; — black Teorī, Ipomæa atropurpurea
s.f. The plant Vernonia (or Serratula, or Conyza) anthelmintica; — Bengal madder, Rubia munjista
s.m. A fragrant and resinous exudation from the plant Amyris agallocha; bdellium
vulg. kāl-yaman, s.m. Name of a prince of the Yavans; a tyrannical Asur, the foe of Krishṇ, destroyed by him by a stratagem.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Dumb; hoarse; inarticulate; soft, sweet (tone), low; weak; — a dumb person, &c.; — a low or soft, or inarticulate tone, humming, buzzing, &c.; — the Indian cuckoo ( = kokil)
subs: or kal-kal, s.m. A confused noise, murmuring or buzz (of a crowd), hum; wrangling, quarrel; — an epithet of Śiva
To make a confused noise; to wrangle, &c.
s.m. A pleasing tone or note; — adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Having a sweet voice or note; — a person who has a sweet voice; the Indian cuckoo ( = kokil); a dove, pigeon, &c.
s.m. (f. -ī), A kind of duck or goose; the whistling teal.
wety: From Sanskrit कल (kala).

def: s.m. The head; crown (of the head); top, upper part; upper leather (of a shoe or slipper); — cheek; jaw; gill (of a fish); throat; (met.) clamour, noise, loud speech; boasting
subs: To have a noisy dispute, to have a duel of words
s.m. A head filled with air; arrogance, haughtiness
(or kallā-) parwār or parwar, s.m. A kind of sweetmeat
s.m. Noise and bustle; pomp and splendour
adj. Lantern-jawed; 'dry-headed,' mad, crazy; (met.) treacherous, cunning, malicious; — s.m. Noise, clamour, tumult
vulg. kalle-darāz, adj. & s.m. Noisy, clamorous; long-tongued; abusive; — a clamourer, one who raises confused cries or shouts; a talkative or noisy person; a brawler (syn. zabān-darāz)
vulg. kalle-darāzī, s.f. Clamour, noisiness; turbulence; abuse
To make a noise, raise a clamour, to shout, &c.

ety: for A. قلعة qalʻat, v.n. fr. قلع 'to remove; to pull down,' &c.
def: s.m. A fort (esp. one on a mountain or an eminence), a fortress, castle, citadel, fortification
subs: To make a fortification, or an entrenchment
To build a fort; to fortify (a place)
adj. Shut up in a fort
To take refuge in a fort; to keep in a fortified place (an army)
vulg. qilʻe-dār, s.m. Governor of a fort, commandant of a garrison
s.f. The command of a garrison
s.f. A siege-gun; siege-battery; a very large cannon
s.f. The garrison of a fort.

ety: said to be act. part. of قلب 'to turn, convert,' &c.; but prob. P. kālab arabicized
def: s.m. A mould, model, form, prototype; (shoemaker's) last; material (of a thing); frame, figure, bust; the body, the lifeless form; — the heart-shaped block on which a tailor cuts out caps; (in Arch.) the centering
subs: To change (one's) body, to be transformed, to undergo transmigration.

ety: = S. कलाप, i.e. कला+आप, fr. rt. आप्
def: s.m. A raw thread as it is wound from the spindle; raw silk wound on a reel; yarn; a skein or ball of thread, a clew, hank; a reel; a wheel (for winding thread on); — (for kalāwā, q.v.) the band round an elephant's neck in which the driver fixes his feet (syn. kalāwa).
s.m. Silk thread covered with gold or silver; silk and silver (or gold) thread twisted together; gold or silver thread; gold or silver fringe.
s.m. Silk thread covered with gold or silver; silk and silver (or gold) thread twisted together; gold or silver thread; gold or silver fringe.

ety: prob. S. कला+वृत्त+ उलःʻ cf. jāmun
def: s.m. Silk thread covered with gold or silver; silk and silver (or gold) thread twisted together; gold or silver thread; gold or silver fringe.
adj. Made of kalābattūn.
s.f. = کلابازي kalā-bāzī, q.v.
s.m. A young elephant; a young camel.
s.m. A small fort; a redoubt.
s.m. = kalap, q.v.

ety: inf. n. of کلف 'to attend carefully to anything,' &c.
def: s.m. Attention, application; — a blackish red or yellow colour; a pimple (on the face); a freckle.
s.m. corr. of qufl, q.v.
s.m. A crow; a raven; a rook
s.m. A little crow, or rook; a magpie.
s.m. See s.v. kāla or kāl, 'black.'
s.m. Strife, contention, quarrel
subs: (˚ha+ās˚), part. adj. Deeply engaged in contention
(f. -ī), or kalah-kārī (f. -iṇī), adj. & s.m. Contentious, quarrelsome, turbulent; — a contentious man, a strife-maker; a brawler, a turbulent fellow
s.f. A contentious or quarrelsome woman; a vixen.

def: s.m. A musical instrument.
s.m. See s.v. kala or kal.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Contentious, quarrelsome, turbulent; — a quarrelsome man, a brawler, a turbulent fellow
subs: s.f. A quarrelsome woman, a termagant ( = kalahī).

def: s.f. A quarrelsome woman; a termagant, scold, vixen.
s.f. Tumbling feats, tumbling ( = kalā-bāzī)
subs: To tumble, to perform tumbling feats.

def: s.f. The fore-arm; wrist; the part between the hoof and the leg (of an animal), fetlock
s.f. Pulse, leguminous seeds (chiefly of the order Phaseolus).
s.f. corr. of قلعي qalaʻī, q.v.

ety: for A. qalʻī, rel. n. fr. qalʻ, 'a mine'
def: s.f. Tin; — a coating of tin given to culinary vessels, &c.; the application of such coating; — whitewashing; — plating, gilding; — varnish; gloss
subs: or qalaʻī jātī rahnā (-kī), The tin coating (of a culinary or other vessel) to be worn off or effaced
s.m. (colloq.) A cant term for the son of a tinman
(-par), To coat (vessels) with tin, to tin; to whitewash; to apply a wash of gold or silver (to), to plate, gild

or qalāʻī ukhaṛnā (-kī), The gilding, &c. to come off (from); the varnish or gloss (of a thing, &c.) to fade; the disguise (of anyone) to be removed; one's real qualities to be laid bare; to be exposed or unmasked; — qalaʼī kholnā (-kī), To remove or open out the gilding or plating, &c. (of); to lay bare the real qualities (of), to expose, unmask
s.m. A tinner of pots and pans; a tinman
s.f. The business of tinning pots and pans.

def: (pl. of qalʻa, after the model of the reg. A. fem. pl.; final h silent being changed to j), s.m. pl. Forts, fortresses, &c.

ety: inf. n. of قلق 'to be restless or uneasy,' &c.
def: s.m. Fretfulness, restlessness, disquietude, discomfort, perturbation, agitation, commotion, anxiety, keen regret
subs: (-meṅ), The disquietude, or regret, &c. (of a thing) to remain, or to rankle (in the mind)
or qalaq honā (with gen. of thing, and dat. of person), To feel anxiety, or pain, or sorrow; to regret; to pine (for, or after).

def: adj. & s.m. Black, &c. ( = kāla); — a black mark, a mole; a freckle; the black part of the eye; a kind of grain; — name of a Rākshas; name of an Asur; — (in Hindī, prob. for S. kālikā), s.f. Blackness; a black mark or spot; a mole; a freckle; a stain; brand, stigma, disgrace; — (in Bīj-g̠aṇit, or Algebra) one of the expressions of quantity or number (corresponding to our a, b, c, x, y, z, &c.)
subs: s.m. A black spot; a stain; stigma, disgrace.

def: s.m. A kind of sweetmeat.
s.m. Blackness; lamp-black, soot, &c. ( = kālik); a black mark or spot; a mole, freckle, &c. ( = kālak)
subs: (kisī-ke nām-par), To stain or sully (the reputation of), to give (one) a bad name, to brand, stigmatize, vilify
(kisī-ke muṅh-meṅ), To put (one) to shame, to mortify; to give (one) annoyance.

ety: corr. of A. کلاح kulāḥ, inf. n. of کلح
def: s.m. Putting on a sour face, grinning.
s.m. Collector (of revenue), the chief revenue officer of a district.
s.f. A collectorate.

s.m. A kind of poison contained in a bulbous root or tuber; a deadly poison; a poison produced at the churning of the ocean (swallowed by Śiva, and causing the blueness of his throat); — poison, venom; rancour, spite, malice.
s.m. = kalār, q.v.
subs: s.f. Duty on spirituous liquors; revenue from the excise
s.m. A distillery; a liquor-shop, public-house, tavern.

def: s.m. = kalārī, q.v.
s.m. Word, speech, discourse; a complete sentence or proposition; composition, work; — disputation; anything said (or to be said) against, objection, question
subs: To speak; to relate; to affirm; to reason, argue, dispute.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic كَلَام (kalām).

def: s.m. A kind of rice.
s.f. A reed (for writing with); — a graft, a slip; — a side-lock (of hair).

ety: fr. qalm, 'paring,' inf. n. of قلم; cf. Gr. κάλαμος; Lat. calamus, and culmus; S. कलम
def: s.f. Cuttings (of trees, &c., for planting), a set, a slip; a section, paragraph (of a chapter in a book); — the upper part of the beard tapering to a point; a kind of firework; — a crystal (as of salts); — qalam uṭhā-kar likhnā, or qalam bar-dāshta likhnā, To write offhand
subs: adj. lit. 'Throwing away the pen'; neglecting (to write); omitting (in writing); — qalam-andāz karnā, v.t. To omit, leave out (in writing)
To make or mend a pen; — to shave the upper part of the beard
adj. Penned, noted down, written, written out or down; inserted, entered, recorded, registered; taken into account
v.t. To take down in writing, to commit to writing, to write; to note down, make an entry of, book, enter, insert; — qalam-band honā, v.n. To be written, to be taken down in writing, &c.
s.f. Insertion, entry; enrolling, enlisting; a writing of distinct heads and items (as a code of instructions, or the articles of an agreement, or a settlement, &c.); signing an agreement; signature
(-par), To draw the pen (through), to strike out; to obliterate, erase; to cancel
s.f. A pen-knife: qalam-jārī, s.f. Enrolling, enlisting, levying of forces
(-kī), 'One's pen to continue to run'; to continue in office or authority

s.m. Pen-case, pen-and-ink-case, a standish; the ensign of the viziership; — qalam-dān denā (-ko), 'To give the pen-case' (to); to confer a post (upon); — to raise to the dignity of vizier or minister
s.m., or qalam-ravī or rauʼī, s.f. Empire, dominion, domain, territories, sovereignty, jurisdiction
s.m. A writer; an engraver; a painter
s.f. Writing; engraving; painting; the art of engraving, &c.; — workmanship; — a species of flowered calico or chintz
v.t. To cut; to cut off; to prune
(-meṅ), To plant or set down slips or cuttings; to graft (in)
(-par), To draw the pen through or across, to obliterate
(One's) pen to befriend, or stand in good stead; to live by (one's) pen
(vulg. qalmeṅ) ćhoṛnā (-meṅ), To leave some hairs in the upper part of the beard
adv. With one stroke of the pen; at once.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَلَم (qalam), borrowed from Arabic قَلَم (qalam), from Ancient Greek κάλαμος (kálamos), from Proto-Indo-European *ḱélh₂m̥ (“reed, straw”).

def: s.f. The pot-herb Convolvulus repens.
adj. Of reed; like a reed; as long as a reed; long; — penned, written, committed to writing; manuscript; tapering (like a qalam); channeled; curled; — grafted; crystallized (saltpetre, &c.)
subs: s.m. Grafted mango; the Bombay mango
s.m. pl. A kind of pastry made of pulse (baṛā), and cut into long slips (qalam)
s.f. The quilled Cassia, i.e. Cinnamon

def: s.m. A chameleon ( = bū-qalamūn, q.v.).
adj. Large, great, big; elder (syn. baṛā, q.v.)
v.t. To mix, mingle, blend; to shake up, jumble together, to shuffle (cards, &c.); — to parch grain ( = kahlānā, q.v.); — v.n. To mix together; to be confused.
s.m. A man of a mixed caste, the son of a Leṭ man and a Tīvar woman.

r: qalandar, kaland, kuland, kanand, kandan, kunda
ety: for orig. کلندر 'a rough unshaped block or log'; fr. kaland or kuland = kanand; see kandan, and kunda
def: s.m. A kind of wandering Muhammadan monk, with shaven head and beard, who abandons everything, wife, friends, and possessions, and travels about; — a bear-dancer; — the shell, or the fly, of a tent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قلندر (qalandar), from Arabic قَلَنْدَر (qalandar), from Classical Persian کلندر (kalandar, “a rough, uncouth man”).

def: s.m. The fly of a tent; — a kind of silk and cotton cloth.
adj. Of or relating to a qalandar, or to the class of qalandars; — s.f. The life or profession of a qalandar; — a kind of tent (having a fly to it).
s.m. A calendar (of crimes).
[prob. kāla+an+gaṛa), s.m. lit. 'Rude or unpolished measure'; a kind of rāg or song.
s.m. (Dakkhanī), corr. of kalindrā, q.v.
s.f. Greatness, largeness; seniority.
adj. & s.m. Struck with a poisoned weapon (an animal).
s.f. See s.v. kāla, 'black.'

s.m. Name of a sacred mountain in Bundelkhand, the modern Kallinjer; also the country adjacent to it; an assemblage of religious mendicants (so called because Kallinjer is one of their meeting-places. Kālaujar is one of the places enumerated in the Vedas among the tapasya-sthānas, or localities suitable for the practice of austere devotions); — an epithet of Śiva.
s.m. Spot, mark; stain; rust; blemish; brand, stigma, reproach, disgrace; accusation, calumny, obloquy, defamation, aspersion; reflection, suspicion
subs: (-ko), To receive the mark or brand of disgrace, &c.; to be branded, &c.
(-ko), To brand, to stigmatize, to defame, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Soiled, stained; rusty; blemished, sullied, defamed, disgraced, calumniated; liable to reproach; — one who has a stain on his character, a disreputable person, &c.
s.m. corr. of kalkī, q.v.
part. (f. -ā), Spotted, soiled, stained, polluted, contaminated, disgraced; calumniated, defamed
s.m. See s.v. kāl, 'time.'
s.m. = kalā-vant, q.v.s.v. kalā.
s.m. (dialec.) Scarcity, lack, want.
s.m. (dialec.) Sorrow, grief, lamentation, wailing.
s.m. A kind of hair-dye; — a kind of starch (syn. manḍī).

s.m. A band, a bundle; a string (of pearls, &c.); a zone or girdle (of laces, or bells, &c.); totality, whole body or collection (of a number of separate things), assemblage, multitude; perfection; a quiver; a peacock's tail.
s.m. = H. kalāp.
s.m. Blackness; the condition of being black.
s.f. The act of caulking (a ship or boat)
v.n. To be vexed; to be grieved, to be in distress or pain; to grieve, repine, fret, lament.

s.m. A class of Hindūs whose profession originally was distilling spirits; an individual of that class; a distiller; a man who extracts the juice of the palmyra and date trees, a toddy-drawer; a seller of spirituous liquors, wine-merchant, vintner, tavern-keeper, inn-keeper (see kalāl).
s.m. A seller of spirituous liquors, &c. ( = kalār).
s.f. A woman of the kalār caste; the wife of a kalār; a woman who keeps a liquour-shop, &c.
s.f. A woman of the kalār caste; the wife of a kalār; a woman who keeps a liquour-shop, &c.
s.m. Blackness ( = kālak).
s.m. = kalas, or kalsā, q.v.
s.m. A water-pot, pitcher, jar; — a rounded pinnacle or ball (or other similar ornament) on the top of a dome; pinnacle, spire, peak, crest; dome, cupola
subs: To make an offering of a jar of water to a deity (a twig of each of the following trees, deemed sacred, having been previously placed in it, 1˚ the aśvattha or Ficus religiosa; 2˚ the baṛ or Ficus indica; 3˚ the udambar or Ficus glomerata; 4˚ the śamī or Mimosa albida; 5˚ the āmra or mango-tree).

def: s.m. = kalas, or kalsā, q.v.
s.f. (dim.) = kalas or kalsā, q.v.
s.f. = kalsī, q.v.; — (also) the plant Hemionitis cordifolia.
v.n. To turn from side to side; to turn back (from one's word or promise).
s.f. A wife.
s.m. (Dakkhanī) = kanauṅṛā, q.v.
s.f. The black aromatic seed of Nigella indica (it is used medicinally; — syn. magrelā).
s.f. (rustic), Blackness, &c. ( = kālak, q.v.).

ety: prob. S. कल्मष; or fr. काल
def: s.f. Blackness; stain, spot, impurity, &c. (see kalmash, and kalaṅk, and dāg̠).
s.m. The band or rope round an elephant's neck in which the driver fixes his feet (cf. kalāba, and kalāp).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀸𑀯 (kalāva), from Sanskrit कलाप (kalāpa). First attested in c. 1603 as Middle Hindi کلاوا (klava). Cognate with Marathi कळवा (kaḷvā), Punjabi کلاوا (kalāvā).

def: s.m. = kalāba, and kalāwā, qq.v.
s.m. See s.v. kalā.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit कलावत् (kalāvat).

def: s.m. A sparrow.
s.m. A long, thin, and small black bean (very glutinous and nutritious: it is used as food for horses).

ety: inf. n. of قلب 'to invert, turn over or upside down,' &c.
def: s.m. Turning, changing; turning over or upside down, inverting; inversion; — the heart; the mind; the secret thoughts; the soul; the intellect, intelligence, understanding; — the main body (of an army); — the pure, or choice, or best part (of anything), pith, marrow, kernel; — counterfeit coin; — adj. Inverted; adulterated, counterfeit, bad; — significant of operations of the mind (a verb, e.g. fěʻluʼl-qalb, or fěʻl-ě-qalb); — strongly fortified, impregnable
subs: s.m. A maker of counterfeit coin, a counterfeit-coiner
v.t. To turn over, to invert, to turn
s.f. Place of the heart; the middle; the middle or centre (of an army).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian قَلْب (qalb), from Arabic قَلْب (qalb).

def: s.m. A dog (syn. sag; kuttā).
wety: Borrowed from English club.

def: s.f. = kalwal, q.v.
s.m. A kind of song or music.
s.m. A variety of the Aloe perfoliata (the Aloe plant from which the aloes of the market are obtained).
adj. Of or relating to the heart; hearty, cordial (syn. dilī); — adulterated; false; counterfeit, base (as coin).
s.m. The arabicized form of kālbud, q.v.

ety: for orig. kalbūt, fr. kalb or kalp = Pehl. karp; Zend kehrp (fr. karep = S. kalp); S. कल्प;+but (?), q.v.
def: s.m. The body (of a man or animal); the frame; the heart; — figure, form, mould, model; a last (for shoes).
adj. = kal-jahwāṅ, q.v.

s.m. The liver (commonly applied to the liver of a human being; the fem. kalejī being used for the liver of an animal); the liver, lungs, and heart collectively (syn. jigar-band), the vitals; the heart; stomach, heart, mind, spirit, courage, magnanimity
subs: The heart to bound or leap (with joy, &c.); to be overjoyed; the heart to palpitate
To lose heart, to be anxious or distracted
The heart to come to the mouth; to be exhausted with excessive vomiting
(-ke), To be wounded in the heart; to suffer a heavy shock or blow
To suffer jealousy or envy; to repine
(-kā, or merā, &c.), To be deeply wounded, to be cut to the quick
(kā), The heart to burst; to feel pity or compassion; to be disturbed with grief; to burst with envy or jealousy
(-kā), 'The heart to be fresh'; to be at ease, to be well off
'To press the heart and weep,' to weep bitterly
To suppress (one's) feelings; to repress grief
To satisfy the heart's longing, to obtain (one's) wish; to get ease
(-kā), 'The heart to burn,' to suffer sorrow, to be heart-sore, to mourn
(-ko), To keep next to the heart, to hold very dear, to love exceedingly, to cherish; to caress, embrace
The heart to tremble or palpitate; to feel afraid; to suffer cold
Liver-complaint; heart-disease: kalejā muṅh-ko ānā = kalejā ulaṭnā; — (also) to feel sick (at the sight of), to loathe, abhor
(-ko), To cherish in the heart, to love or esteem exceedingly
or kaleje-khāʼī, s.f. A witch who devours the livers of human beings.

def: s.m. = kalejā, q.v.
s.f. The liver (of an animal); the liver, lungs, and heart together (syn. jigar-band).
s.m. = kalakṭar, q.v.
s.m. (rustic), 1˚ = karelā; — 2˚ = kalewā, qq.v.
s.m. See kleś.
s.m. (poet.) = kaleś or kleś, q.v.
s.m. Name of a drug.
adj. & s.m. (f. -hāʼī), Troublesome, giving annoyance; quarrelsome; — a troublesome person, or a quarrelsome person.
s.m. Name of a drug.
s.m. = kalewā, q.v.
s.m. A light morning meal, breakfast; luncheon, stale food
subs: To make a morning meal, to breakfast, &c.

def: s.m. The body; a carcass.
s.m. = کلغا kalg̠ā, q.v.

ety: for kalgā, kalgī; prob. = S. कलश, कलशी, by change of ś to k, and then to g̠; — P. kalakī
def: s.m. A composer or singer of the kalg̠ī or lāʼonī songs.
s.f. = kalgī, q.v.
s.f. = کلغي kalg̠ī, q.v.

ety: for kalgā, kalgī; prob. = S. कलश, कलशी, by change of ś to k, and then to g̠; — P. kalakī
def: s.m. A composer or singer of the kalg̠ī or lāʼonī songs.
adv. Yesterday; to-morrow.
adv. (rustic) = kalh, or kal, q.v.
s.f. A woollen carpet; tapestry ( = qālīn).

s.m. A die; the game of dice personified; name of the last and worst of the four yugas or ages, the present or iron age (according to the Hindūs), the age of vice (the Kali or fourth age contains 432,000 years of men, and is supposed to begin on the 18th February 3102 B.C.; at the end of this yuga the world is to be destroyed); — dissension, strife, contention, quarrel
subs: vulg. kal-kāl, s.m. The time or age of Kali, the fourth age (in the Hindū Mythology)
s.m. An epithet of any poet of the fourth or Kali-yug
vulg. kal-mal, s.m. Impurity, guilt, sin
s.f. An epithet of the river Karam-nāsā (q.v.)
vulg. kali-jug, or kal-jug, s.m. The fourth or iron age
vulg. kal-jugī, adj. Of or relating to the fourth or iron age; extending over the period of the fourth age.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀺𑀕 (kaliga), from Sanskrit कलिका (kalikā). Cognate with Gujarati કળી (kaḷī), Marathi कळी (kaḷī).

ety: fem. of काल; and of H. kālā
def: s.f. Black colour; blacking; ink; a black spot, a mole, a freckle, &c. ( = kālak); — an epithet of the goddess Durgā, wife of Śiva (the Hecate of the Hindūs, to whom human sacrifices are offered); — the river Nile.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit काली (kālī). Equivalent to کال (kāl) + ـی (-ī).

def: adv. (rustic) = kalh, or kal, q.v.

s.f. An unblown flower, a bud, blossom, button (of a flower); — new feather (of a bird); a gusset, a piece put in a garment (to enlarge it); — quick-lime, wet-lime; — a kind of pipe or ḥuqqa (cf. kaliyān)
subs: (-par), To whitewash; to coat with lime
(-meṅ), To have buds; to bud, blossom; — to get new feathers, to begin to be fledged.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀓𑀮𑀺𑀕 (kaliga), from Sanskrit कलिका (kalikā). Cognate with Gujarati કળી (kaḷī), Marathi कळी (kaḷī).

def: s.m. Name of a Nāga or serpent that inhabited the Jamnā (it is said to have had a hundred and ten heads, and to have been slain by Krishṇ)
subs: or kālī-kunḍ, s.m. The pit or whirlpool in the river Jamnā in which the serpent Kālī is said to have lived

def: adj. Cast in a mould, moulded.
s.m. A small carpet; a rug.

ety: kālī+dāsa, 'the slave of Kālī or Durgā'
def: s.m. Name of a celebrated Hindū poet (the Shakespeare of India; author of the Śakuntalā, Vikramorvaśī, Megha-dūta, Raghu-vaṅśa, Nalodaya, and other poems: he was one of the nine poets or gems of Vikramāditya's court, and is supposed to have flourished in the century preceding the Christian era); a proper name.
s.m. See s.v. کلي kali.

s.f. Blackness; black spot; blacking; ink; lamp-black; soot, the smut which collects under pots, &c.; cloudiness, darkness, gloom; — a fault or flaw (in gold); the line of hair extending from the pudenda to the navel; — the small singing-bird Turdus macrourus (com. called shāmā); a female crow; a black curlew.
s.m. A curlew; — adj. Of or relating to kali-yug.
s.f. Price to be paid at a fixed period or by instalments
s.f. A digit or the sixteenth part of the moon; — an unblown flower ( = kalī).
adj. (f. -ā or -ī), Relating to time, connected with time; (in Gram.) relating to tense; temporal; seasonable; periodical.
s.f. Blackness; ink, &c. (see kālik); an epithet of the goddess Durgā ( = kālī, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ā), Covered (with), full (of), filled; mixed, blended; impenetrable, impervious; — s.m. A large heap; a thicket; confusion; mud.

ety: v.n. fr. قلّ 'to be little, or few,' &c.; see qillat
def: adj. Little, small, scanty, deficient; moderate; a few; few, rare; spare, lean; short
subs: adj. Small and large or great; little or much, more or less, &c. ( = thoṛā-bahut)
v.n. To be little; to be deficient, to fall short (of).

ety: prob. fr. ii of کلم; see kalām
def: s.m. One who speaks to another, an interlocutor ( = kalām karne-wālā); a familiar
subs: One who speaks to God; an epithet of Moses.

def: s.f. Word, saying, &c.
adj. Belonging or relating to any particular time; timely, seasonable; old, ancient.
s.f. A woollen or Turkey carpet.
s.m. Name of the mountain on which the Jamnā rises.
s.m. The water-melon (syn. tarbūz).
s.f. An epithet of the river Yamuna or Jamnā; — name of a wife of Krishṇ; name of the wife of Asita and mother of Sagara.
s.m. = kalindā, q.v.
s.f. The plant Ipomæa turpethum (the bark of which is used as a purgative; — syn. teʼorī).

s.m. Name of a country or district on the Coromandel coast extending from below Cuttack (Kaṭak) to the vicinity of Madras (the name is applied in the Purāṇas to several other places also); — the febrifuge-nut plant Cœsalpinia (or Guilandina) bonducella; the plant Pongamia glabra ( = karanjā).
s.m. A Christian church; a synagogue.
s.m. A Christian church; a synagogue.

ety: prob. kal = S. कला+S. aff. इतः
def: part. adj. 'Machine-made'; fabricated, contrived; beautiful.
adj. Black, dark-complexioned.
s.m. Name of a Nāga or serpent that inhabited the Jamnā (it is said to have had a hundred and ten heads, and to have been slain by Krishṇ)
subs: or kālī-kunḍ, s.m. The pit or whirlpool in the river Jamnā in which the serpent Kālī is said to have lived

ety: for A. قلية qalīyat, v.n. fr. قلی 'to cook, fry,' &c.
def: s.m. Broiled flesh-meat dressed with anything; a curry; — ashes of which soap is made; potash.
s.m. A small simple kind of pipe or ḥuqqa ( = kalī).
s.m. A pipe for smoking through water, a kind of ḥuqqa.
v.n. To bud, blossom; — to begin to be fledged (a bird); to get new feathers.
s.m. The black kite.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), 'Black-tongued,' inimical, malignant, whose curses prevail; — an evil-tongued person; one whose curses prevail.
adj. (dialec.) = kal-jibhā, q.v.
adj. (f. -īṅ), Dark-complexioned, sallow, tawny.

s.m. The viscous deposit of oily substances; sediment; dregs; paste; ear-wax; filth, dirt, ordure; foulness; sin; fraud, falsehood, deceit, hypocrisy; the beleric myrobalan, Terminalia bellerica; — adj. Sinful, wicked.
s.m. (dialec.), Sediment, &c. ( = kalk); lamp-black.
s.f. A plume, &c. ( = kalg̠ī, q.v.); — (local) a kind of instrument for smoking with.

s.m. Name of Vishṇu in his future capacity of destroyer of the wicked and liberator of the world from its enemies (this is to constitute the tenth or last avatār or descent of the deity, and is to take place at the end of the four yugas or ages; the god will appear as a Brāhman who will be born in the town of Sambhal and in the family of Vishṇu Śarmā).
s.m. A confused or muffled sound; bustle, stir, commotion, tumult.

ety: prob. fr. S. कल् 'to collect,' and akin to kalī
def: s.m. A bud, a sprout; a cabbage ( = karam-kallā); (met.) the head (see کله kalla)
subs: s.m. The head and feet (of an animal); name of a dish.
wety: From Sanskrit कील (kīl).

def: s.m. A dog-keeper.
corr. of قلاچ qallāć or قلاش qallāsh, q.v.

adj. & s.m. Shrewd, cunning, crafty; malicious; — poor, wretched, friendless; — addicted to drinking; — a cunning or crafty person; a needy, worthless fellow; a poor wretch; — a drunkard, a haunter of taverns.
v.n. To burn; to be inflamed (as the skin by rubbing pepper, &c. on it).
adj. = qallāć, q.v.
adj. & s.m. Barren, sterile, brackish, saline (land or soil); saline, salt (food); — barren spot; — saline soil; land which produces saltpetre or reh; — a kind of fuller's earth
subs: s.f. Land impregnated with saltpetre or reh, barren soil
v.n. To be impregnated with alkali or reh; to become barren (land).

def: adj. & s.m. Shrewd, cunning, crafty; malicious; — poor, wretched, friendless; — addicted to drinking; — a cunning or crafty person; a needy, worthless fellow; a poor wretch; — a drunkard, a haunter of taverns.
s.f. Poverty, wretchedness; — drunkenness, revelry.
s.m. A surge, a billow; — joy, happiness, pleasure.
s.f. A river with large waves.

ety: for S. कालिमा, nom. of कालिमन्
def: s.m. Blackness; a black spot, stain, slur, blot; shame and confusion (as of a convicted offender); suspicion; calumny.

ety: for A. کلمة kalimat; see kalām
def: s.m. A word, speech, saying, discourse; a vocable, part of speech; the Mohammadan confession of faith (i.e. lā ilāha illaʼl-lāh, &c., 'There is no god but God,' &c.)
subs: (-ko), To make (one) repeat the (Muslim) confession of faith, to convert (one) to Mohammadanism, to make a Musalmān of (one)
To repeat the (Muslim) confession of faith; — to acknowledge the superiority (of); to say what another says, to follow servilely, to swear (by)
Blasphemous word or speech, blasphemy, profanity
s.m. A Musalmān or Muslim; — a well-wisher, one who sings the praises (of), or one who takes another as his oracle.

def: s.f. See s.v. kal, 'ease,' &c.
adj. & s.m. = kalmāsh, q.v.
s.m. = kalmash, q.v.
s.f. Being in a flutter, fluttering, tossing about, restlessness, agitation, perturbation, disquietude; exhaustion, lassitude.
v.n. To flutter, toss about, flounder; to writhe, wriggle; to fidget; to be restless or uneasy (syn. kulbulānā).

ety: for P. qalam-ān, pl. of qalam
def: s.f. (local), The upper part of the beard, on each side of the face, shaved taper, like pencils.
s.m. Sediment, dregs; dirt; stain, spot; impurity; guilt, sin.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Variegated, spotted; black and white; black; — a variegated colour; a mixture of black and white; — the colour black; — a rākshas, demon, or goblin; a stain; a species of fragrant rice; — name of a Nāga; name of a servant of the Sun (identified with Yama); — name of Śākya-muni in a former birth.
s.f. The spotted cow of Jamadagni, the giver of all desires; — name of a river.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Black-faced; — a black-faced person or animal; — a forbidding or ill-omened countenance.
s.f. Play, sport, frolic, gambol; friskiness, wantonness; recreation, diversion, pleasure, enjoyment
(cf. kalol), s.f.(m.?) A heifer.

s.m. Sacred precept, rule, law, ordinance; rite, ceremony; performance, manner of acting, practice prescribed by the Vedas; the most complete of the six Vedāngas (comprehending the description of religious rites); a śāstra or sacred book of the Hindūs; — a resolve, resolution, determination, purpose; propriety, fitness; optionality, alternative; doubt; toxicology; — a fabulous period of time, a day of Brahma or 1000 yugas, a period of 4,320,000,000 years (measuring the duration of the world); the duration of the world; the destruction of the world; — an epithet of Vishṇu (as containing all the sacred precepts; — name of one of the trees of Indra's paradise (see kalp-taru and kalp-vr̤iksh)
subs: (˚pa+an˚), s.m. The end of a kalpa or period of the world's duration, the end of the world
s.m. & adv. Another kalp; — in the midst of a kalp; from kalp to kalp
s.m., or kalp-latā, s.f., or kalp-vr̤iksh, vulg. kalp-br̤iksh, s.m. One of the fabulous plants or trees of Indra's paradise, a tree that grants all desires
s.m. The final destruction, the end of the world.

def: s.f. Fashioning, fabrication; contrivance, scheme, plan, invention; imitation, forgery; — inference, supposition, hypothesis, conjecture; fancy; imagination, thought, conception; — keen or ardent desire; trouble, difficulty
subs: v.t. To fashion, form, make, fabricate, contrive, &c.; to suppose, &c.

def: v.t. To grieve, distress, pain.
adj. Existing only in fancy; unreal, fictitious, invented, contrived; artificial; counterfeit; fabricated; feigned, hypocritical.
s.f. = S کالپنکتو काल्पनिकत्व kālpanikatva, vulg. kālpniktwā, s.m. Contrivance; forgery; hypocrisy, dissimulation.
s.m. = S کالپنکتا काल्पनिकता kālpanikatā, vulg. kālpniktā, s.f. Contrivance; forgery; hypocrisy, dissimulation.
part. (f. ā), Made, prepared, fabricated, artificial, contrived, false, artificially produced or constructed; — supposed, inferred; assumed, given, granted.
v.t. To fashion, fabricate, &c. ( = kalpanā karnā, q.v.)
subs: to starch; to dye the hair
